title: Standpipe Operation with Reusable Overlay Labels
scene: Engine 181 is supplying the FDC for a standpipe operation in a multi-story apartment building. The engine connects to the FDC at street level. The standpipe system carries water vertically to the fire floor. Once the standpipe reaches the assigned floor, a 2½″ attack hose of the listed length is connected to the standpipe outlet and advances to the nozzle.
studentQuestion: Calculate the pump pressure for the standpipe setup. Engine 181 supplies the FDC, the standpipe rises to the fire floor, and the attack hose is connected to the standpipe outlet on that floor.
details: Standpipe system rises vertically to the fire floor
Standpipe outlet on the fire floor
2½″ attack hose connected at the standpipe outlet
Attack hose advances from standpipe outlet to nozzle
formulaBreakdown: Standpipe carries water vertically to Floor 3
Attack hose connected at Floor 3 standpipe outlet = 150′ of 2½″ hose
PP = 21 FL + 50 NP + 15 elevation + 25 system loss = 111 psi
instructorExplanation: For this standpipe operation, the engine pumps into the FDC and the standpipe carries water up to the fire floor. Once at the fire floor, calculate friction loss only in the attack hose connected to the standpipe outlet, then add nozzle pressure, elevation, and standpipe system loss.
explainMistake: Common mistakes are treating the standpipe riser as attack hose, forgetting the 25 psi standpipe system loss, using the wrong C value for 2½″ hose, using the wrong smooth bore flow, or missing the elevation pressure for the fire floor.

variations.change: Base question: standpipe rises to Floor 3, then 150′ of 2½″ hose is connected to the 3rd floor standpipe outlet with a 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle.
variations.question: Engine 181 is supplying the FDC. The standpipe rises to Floor 3. A 150′ section of 2½″ hose is connected to the 3rd floor standpipe outlet and ends in a 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle. What pump pressure should you set?
details: 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the 3rd floor standpipe outlet
formulaBreakdown: Attack hose connected to 3rd floor standpipe outlet = 150′ of 2½″ hose
PP = 21 FL + 50 NP + 15 elevation + 25 system loss
variations.instructorExplanation: Use friction loss only in the 150′ attack hose connected to the 3rd floor standpipe outlet. Then add 50 psi nozzle pressure, 15 psi elevation, and 25 psi standpipe system loss.

variations.change: Increase the attack hose length connected at the 3rd floor standpipe outlet to 200′. Keep the 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle and Floor 3.
variations.question: Engine 181 is supplying the FDC. The standpipe rises to Floor 3. A 200′ section of 2½″ hose is connected to the 3rd floor standpipe outlet and ends in a 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle. What pump pressure should you set?
details: 200′ of 2½″ hose connected at the 3rd floor standpipe outlet
formulaBreakdown: Attack hose connected to 3rd floor standpipe outlet = 200′ of 2½″ hose
PP = 28 FL + 50 NP + 15 elevation + 25 system loss
variations.instructorExplanation: Only the attack hose length changed. Calculate friction loss in the 200′ hose connected to the standpipe outlet, then add nozzle pressure, elevation, and system loss.

variations.change: Move the standpipe outlet and attack hose connection to Floor 5. Keep 150′ of 2½″ hose and the 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle.
variations.question: Engine 181 is supplying the FDC. The standpipe rises to Floor 5. A 150′ section of 2½″ hose is connected to the 5th floor standpipe outlet and ends in a 1⅛″ smooth bore nozzle. What pump pressure should you set?
details: 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the 5th floor standpipe outlet
formulaBreakdown: Attack hose connected to 5th floor standpipe outlet = 150′ of 2½″ hose
PP = 21 FL + 50 NP + 25 elevation + 25 system loss
variations.instructorExplanation: Only the standpipe height changed. The attack hose is now connected on Floor 5, so the elevation pressure is higher.

variations.change: Change the nozzle to a 15/16″ smooth bore. Keep the standpipe to Floor 3 and 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the 3rd floor outlet.
variations.question: Engine 181 is supplying the FDC. The standpipe rises to Floor 3. A 150′ section of 2½″ hose is connected to the 3rd floor standpipe outlet and ends in a 15/16″ smooth bore nozzle. What pump pressure should you set?
details: 150′ of 2½″ hose connected at the 3rd floor standpipe outlet
formulaBreakdown: Attack hose connected to 3rd floor standpipe outlet = 150′ of 2½″ hose
PP = 10 FL + 50 NP + 15 elevation + 25 system loss
variations.instructorExplanation: The standpipe still rises to Floor 3 and the hose is still connected there. Only the nozzle changed, so the flow and friction loss are lower.
